business-case.title: Business Case (PMO/BA - Pre-Initiation)
business-case.description: Justifies why the project should exist by defining the problem, expected value, options, costs, risks, and success measures.

fields.label: Initiative Name
fields.placeholder: e.g., Customer Onboarding Modernization

fields.label: Business Problem or Opportunity
fields.placeholder: What is happening today that requires investment?

fields.label: Expected Benefits and Value
fields.placeholder: What value, outcomes, or benefits are expected?

fields.label: Options Considered
fields.placeholder: List the main options, including doing nothing.

fields.label: Cost and Investment Summary
fields.placeholder: Estimate cost, timing, funding, and major constraints.

project-charter.title: Project Charter (PM - Initiation)
project-charter.description: Formally authorizes the project and establishes its purpose, scope boundaries, leadership, and high-level success conditions.

fields.label: Project Name
fields.placeholder: e.g., E-Commerce Checkout Optimization

fields.label: Project Objective
fields.placeholder: What business outcome will this project achieve?

fields.label: High-Level Scope
fields.placeholder: Summarize what is in and out of scope.

fields.label: Project Sponsor
fields.placeholder: Executive sponsor name

fields.label: Success Criteria
fields.placeholder: How will the project be judged successful?

stakeholder-register.title: Stakeholder Register and Engagement Plan (PM/BA - Initiation)
stakeholder-register.description: Identifies stakeholders, their interests, influence, impacts, and the approach for engaging them throughout the project.

fields.label: Stakeholder Groups
fields.placeholder: List key stakeholder groups, roles, and representatives.

fields.label: Needs, Concerns, and Expectations
fields.placeholder: What matters most to each major stakeholder group?

fields.label: Influence and Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: Who has high influence, high impact, or decision authority?

fields.label: Engagement Strategy
fields.placeholder: How will the team engage, align, and escalate with stakeholders?

fields.label: Communication and Review Cadence
fields.placeholder: Describe touchpoints, reporting rhythm, and forums.

integrated-project-plan.title: Integrated Project Plan (PMO - Planning)
integrated-project-plan.description: Provides the working plan for delivery, including approach, milestones, governance, controls, and execution rhythms.

fields.label: Delivery Approach
fields.placeholder: Describe the lifecycle, work model, and major phases.

fields.label: Major Milestones
fields.placeholder: List major milestones, stage gates, and target dates.

fields.label: Governance Model
fields.placeholder: Define meetings, approvals, decision rights, and escalation paths.

fields.label: Controls and Baselines
fields.placeholder: Summarize scope, schedule, cost, quality, and reporting controls.

fields.label: Delivery Team and Roles
fields.placeholder: Who is accountable, responsible, consulted, and informed?

scope-statement.title: Scope Statement and Deliverables Baseline (PM/BA - Planning)
scope-statement.description: Defines the detailed project and product scope, key deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, constraints, and exclusions.

fields.label: Product or Service Scope
fields.placeholder: Describe the product, service, or capability being delivered.

fields.label: Major Deliverables
fields.placeholder: List the major outputs the project must produce.

fields.label: Acceptance Criteria
fields.placeholder: What conditions must be met for acceptance?

fields.label: Assumptions and Constraints
fields.placeholder: Document key planning assumptions and constraints.

fields.label: Explicit Exclusions
fields.placeholder: State what is out of scope to reduce ambiguity.

requirements-package.title: Requirements Package / BRD (BA - Elicitation and Analysis)
requirements-package.description: Captures business, stakeholder, functional, and non-functional requirements in a structured package that can drive solution definition and delivery.

fields.label: Business Requirements
fields.placeholder: What business outcomes, capabilities, or rules are required?

fields.label: Stakeholder and User Requirements
fields.placeholder: What do users, operators, and stakeholders need?

fields.label: Functional Requirements
fields.placeholder: What must the solution do?

fields.label: Non-Functional Requirements
fields.placeholder: Document security, performance, reliability, audit, and compliance needs.

fields.label: Business Rules and Constraints
fields.placeholder: List governing rules, policies, and required constraints.

requirements-traceability.title: Requirements Traceability Matrix (BA/PM - Control)
requirements-traceability.description: Connects requirements to objectives, design elements, delivery items, test coverage, decisions, and status so scope can be managed end to end.

fields.label: Traceability Approach
fields.placeholder: How will requirements be linked across the lifecycle?

fields.label: Requirement Sources
fields.placeholder: Where do requirements originate and who owns them?

fields.label: Mapping Rules
fields.placeholder: How will requirements map to design, build, and test artifacts?

fields.label: Change and Version Handling
fields.placeholder: How will traceability be maintained when requirements change?

fields.label: Coverage Gaps and Risks
fields.placeholder: What gaps, orphaned requirements, or coverage risks exist?

raid-log.title: RAID Log (PM - Delivery Control)
raid-log.description: Tracks risks, assumptions, issues, and dependencies that require active monitoring and action during execution.

fields.label: Key Risks
fields.placeholder: List the top risks with owners and response strategies.

fields.label: Critical Assumptions
fields.placeholder: Document assumptions that could affect delivery if invalid.

fields.label: Open Issues
fields.placeholder: What issues need resolution, by whom, and by when?

fields.label: Dependencies
fields.placeholder: What external or internal dependencies could affect delivery?

fields.label: Immediate Actions
fields.placeholder: What actions must be taken next and who owns them?

change-decision-log.title: Change and Decision Log (PMO - Governance)
change-decision-log.description: Maintains the record of approved and pending changes, key decisions, rationale, impacts, and follow-through actions.

fields.label: Change Requests
fields.placeholder: Describe requested changes, impacts, and current status.

fields.label: Decision Register
fields.placeholder: Capture decisions, dates, approvers, and rationale.

fields.label: Impact Assessment
fields.placeholder: Summarize impact to scope, timeline, cost, quality, and risk.

fields.label: Approval Path
fields.placeholder: Who approves what, and under what thresholds?

fields.label: Follow-Up Actions
fields.placeholder: What implementation actions were created by each decision?

architecture-vision.title: Architecture Vision (Architecture - Initiation)
architecture-vision.description: Defines the target architectural intent, stakeholder concerns, scope, principles, and the high-level end state the project aims to achieve.

fields.label: Architecture Problem or Driver
fields.placeholder: What strategic, business, or technical driver is shaping the architecture effort?

fields.label: Stakeholder Concerns
fields.placeholder: What concerns must the architecture address for sponsors, users, and operators?

fields.label: Target State Summary
fields.placeholder: Describe the high-level future architecture and expected outcomes.

fields.label: Principles and Constraints
fields.placeholder: What architecture principles, standards, and constraints apply?

fields.label: Scope Boundaries
fields.placeholder: What domains, systems, and teams are in or out of scope?

architecture-requirements.title: Architecture Requirements Specification (Architecture/BA - Planning)
architecture-requirements.description: Consolidates architecture-significant requirements, constraints, assumptions, and quality attributes that the solution design must satisfy.

fields.label: Quality Attributes
fields.placeholder: Document availability, resilience, performance, supportability, and similar qualities.

fields.label: Security and Compliance Requirements
fields.placeholder: Capture security, privacy, audit, and regulatory requirements.

fields.label: Integration and Data Constraints
fields.placeholder: What interfaces, data rules, and interoperability constraints exist?

fields.label: Technical Constraints and Standards
fields.placeholder: List standards, platforms, reuse expectations, and prohibitions.

fields.label: Architecture Risks and Trade-Offs
fields.placeholder: What key design tensions or architecture risks need explicit attention?

solution-architecture.title: Solution Architecture Definition (Architecture - Design)
solution-architecture.description: Documents the end-to-end solution structure across business, application, data, integration, and technology viewpoints.

fields.label: Solution Context
fields.placeholder: Describe the solution context, actors, systems, and boundaries.

fields.label: Logical Design Overview
fields.placeholder: Summarize key components, services, data flows, and responsibilities.

fields.label: Deployment and Environment View
fields.placeholder: How will the solution be deployed across environments and platforms?

fields.label: Integration and Information View
fields.placeholder: Describe interfaces, data entities, events, and integration patterns.

fields.label: Key Design Decisions
fields.placeholder: Record major design decisions, rationale, and open architecture actions.

transition-migration-plan.title: Transition and Migration Plan (PM/Architecture - Delivery)
transition-migration-plan.description: Defines how the target solution will be implemented, released, adopted, and transitioned into operations with manageable risk.

fields.label: Release Strategy
fields.placeholder: Describe rollout waves, cutover approach, and sequencing.

fields.label: Migration Dependencies
fields.placeholder: What dependencies, prerequisites, and readiness criteria exist?

fields.label: Operational Readiness
fields.placeholder: What support, training, monitoring, and handover activities are required?

fields.label: Rollback and Contingency
fields.placeholder: How will rollback, fallback, and contingency be handled?

fields.label: Adoption and Change Plan
fields.placeholder: How will affected users and teams adopt the new solution?

test-acceptance-plan.title: Test and Acceptance Plan (BA/QA/PM - Validation)
test-acceptance-plan.description: Defines how the team will verify requirements, validate business outcomes, and secure formal stakeholder acceptance.

fields.label: Test Scope
fields.placeholder: What will be tested and what will not?

fields.label: Requirements Coverage
fields.placeholder: How will business and solution requirements be covered by testing?

fields.label: Entry and Exit Criteria
fields.placeholder: What conditions govern the start and completion of test phases?

fields.label: Acceptance Approach
fields.placeholder: How will UAT, business sign-off, and defect thresholds be handled?

fields.label: Roles, Environments, and Schedule
fields.placeholder: Who participates, where will testing occur, and when?

closure-lessons-learned.title: Closure Report and Lessons Learned (PMO - Closeout)
closure-lessons-learned.description: Summarizes delivery outcomes, acceptance, benefits outlook, unresolved items, and lessons to carry into future work.

fields.label: Delivery Summary
fields.placeholder: What was delivered and what remains open or deferred?

fields.label: Acceptance and Handover
fields.placeholder: Document approvals, handover status, and operational ownership.

fields.label: Benefits and Outcome Outlook
fields.placeholder: What benefits are expected now, and how will they be monitored?

fields.label: Lessons Learned
fields.placeholder: What should future teams repeat, change, or avoid?

fields.label: Follow-On Actions
fields.placeholder: List any post-project actions, owners, and target dates.
